howToBuy.heroTitle: How to Buy — Your Complete Sourcing Guide
howToBuy.heroDesc: BuildReach connects East African buyers with verified Chinese building material suppliers. We handle sourcing, quality inspection, and consolidated shipping — so you can buy Chinese building materials as easily as local ones.
howToBuy.step1Desc: Explore 10+ building material categories. Filter by specs, certifications, and supply mode. Add products to your inquiry cart.
howToBuy.step1Detail: Use keyword search, category navigation, or parameter filters. Can't find what you need? Submit a custom requirement directly.
howToBuy.step2Desc: Fill in your Request for Quotation online: select products, specify quantities and destination port. You can also upload images, BOM lists, or text descriptions.
howToBuy.step2Detail: After submission, our team will prepare a sourcing plan with supplier quotes within 1-3 business days.
howToBuy.step3Desc: Receive a detailed quotation including: unit price, MOQ, estimated lead time, and product certifications. Review each item online.
howToBuy.step3Detail: Quotes are based on multi-supplier price comparison. Supplier identities and raw costs are not disclosed — we ensure you get the best deal.
howToBuy.step4Desc: Accept the quotation to generate a purchase order. Pay 30% deposit to lock in your order. We immediately start procurement from suppliers.
howToBuy.step4Detail: Track your order status anytime in 'My Orders'. All product details, prices, and specs are snapshot-saved at order creation.
howToBuy.step5Desc: We procure from suppliers on your behalf, arrange factory QC inspection, and warehouse goods after they pass. Track procurement and QC status per line item.
howToBuy.step5Detail: Failed QC items are returned to suppliers for replacement, ensuring quality before shipment.
howToBuy.step6Desc: Once ready, goods are packed into shared containers by destination port. Multiple orders share one container, cutting international freight costs significantly.
howToBuy.step6Detail: Ships depart from Ningbo/Yiwu to Dar es Salaam, ~25-30 days transit. Full logistics timeline visible in your order details.
howToBuy.step7Desc: Pay the remaining 70% balance before port arrival. We assist with destination customs clearance. Confirm receipt to complete the order.
howToBuy.step7Detail: Need import permits (e.g. TBS certification)? Let us know in your RFQ and we can help prepare the required documents.
howToBuy.adv1Desc: Source directly from Chinese factories, cutting middlemen for factory prices
howToBuy.adv2Desc: Pre-shipment QC inspection on every batch; failed items replaced before shipping
howToBuy.adv3Desc: LCL consolidation: share containers with other buyers, saving 60%-70% on freight
howToBuy.adv4Desc: Procurement, QC, customs, shipping, and clearance — all managed for you
howToBuy.faq1Q: What is the minimum order quantity (MOQ)?
howToBuy.faq1A: Varies by product category. Most building materials have low MOQs, further reduced by our consolidation model. Specific MOQ is stated in each quotation.
howToBuy.faq2Q: How long from order to delivery?
howToBuy.faq2A: Typically 45-60 days: ~15-20 days production + ~25-30 days sea freight + ~5-7 days customs. Exact timing depends on product lead time and shipping schedule.
howToBuy.faq3A: Yes. Note 'sample needed' in your RFQ and we'll coordinate with suppliers. Sample and shipping costs are borne by the buyer.
howToBuy.faq4A: Currently bank transfer (T/T). 30% deposit + 70% before port arrival. More payment options coming soon.
howToBuy.faq5Q: How about customs clearance and import permits?
howToBuy.faq5A: We provide all export documents (packing list, invoice, certificate of origin, etc.). Import clearance and TBS certification assistance available — mention it in your RFQ.
howToBuy.faq6Q: What if there are quality issues after delivery?
howToBuy.faq6A: We inspect every batch before shipment. If issues are found after receipt, contact us within 7 days and we'll coordinate with the supplier to resolve.

fulfillment.heroSub: Group sourcing · LCL consolidation · Unified customs · Ocean freight
fulfillment.sectionProcessSub: From order placement to port delivery, every step guaranteed
fulfillment.step_P1_desc: We screen suppliers through factory audits, qualification reviews, and sample testing to ensure quality at the source.
fulfillment.step_P2_desc: Submit your requirements online. We aggregate quotes from multiple suppliers and help you confirm the best option.
fulfillment.step_P3_desc: Once you confirm the quote, the platform generates a fulfillment order locking in price, quantity, and delivery date.
fulfillment.step_1_desc: Suppliers produce according to the fulfillment order. We track production progress with real-time milestone updates.
fulfillment.step_2_desc: Third-party inspectors perform on-site quality checks against international standards. Defective goods are intercepted.
fulfillment.step_3_desc: Goods from multiple suppliers are consolidated in our warehouse, professionally packed to maximize container space and reduce per-unit freight.
fulfillment.step_4_desc: We handle export customs clearance with complete documentation — packing lists, invoices, certificates of origin — all in one stop.
fulfillment.step_5_desc: Full container shipped to Dar es Salaam port with end-to-end tracking. Transit time approx. 25-30 days.
fulfillment.step_6_desc: Cargo arrives at Dar es Salaam port. We coordinate port operations and arrange unloading into the warehouse.
fulfillment.step_7_desc: We assist with Tanzania import clearance, handling duties, VAT, and all tax declarations.
fulfillment.step_8_desc: After customs clearance, goods are delivered to your designated location. Full-chain service complete.
fulfillment.consolidationTitle: Small orders, full containers — LCL made easy
fulfillment.consolidation_point1_desc: Goods from different suppliers consolidated in one container. Even small orders can ship.
fulfillment.consolidation_point2_desc: Specialized reinforcement for heavy building materials — moisture-proof, shock-proof, reducing damage during transit.
fulfillment.consolidation_point3_desc: Shared container freight saves over 30% compared to individual shipments.
fulfillment.consolidation_point4_desc: Fixed consolidation cycles, 2-3 shipments per month, with predictable arrival dates.
fulfillment.certSub: All exported products meet East African destination country certification requirements
fulfillment.boundaryDesc: BuildReach provides full-chain service from Chinese factories to Dar es Salaam port, including supplier screening, group procurement, QC inspection, consolidation, customs declaration, ocean freight, and destination clearance. Inland transport (port to site) is arranged by the buyer or local logistics. The platform does not provide financial lending, insurance brokerage, or other financial services.

helpCenter.pageDesc: Practical purchasing knowledge organized by category — how to choose materials, assess quality, and determine specifications.
helpCenter.contactHint: Can't find the answer you're looking for?
helpCenter.platform_subtitle: Registration, browsing, quote requests, ordering, and other basic operations
helpCenter.platform_a1: BuildReach is a one-stop cross-border procurement platform for building materials and industrial products, connecting quality Chinese manufacturers with East African buyers. The platform offers product browsing, online quote requests (RFQ), quotation confirmation, compliance documentation coordination, container consolidation, and shipping services to help you complete cross-border procurement efficiently and securely.
helpCenter.platform_q2: How do I register an account?
helpCenter.platform_a2: Click "Sign Up Free" in the top right corner of the page. Fill in your email, phone number, and password to complete registration. No approval is required — you can browse products and submit quote requests immediately after signing up.
helpCenter.platform_q3: How do I search and browse products?
helpCenter.platform_a3:
You can find products in the following ways:
• Enter keywords in the search bar at the top
• Browse by six major categories on the homepage
• Use the category navigation panel on the left to filter step by step
Each product detail page displays specifications, product images, and detailed descriptions to help you quickly understand the product.
helpCenter.platform_q4: What is an RFQ (Request for Quotation)? How do I submit one?
helpCenter.platform_a4:
An RFQ (Request for Quotation) is a procurement request you submit to the platform. You can:
• Click "Add to RFQ Basket" on a product detail page, then submit all items together
• Go directly to the "My RFQs" page to create a new quote request
After submission, the platform will promptly confirm the details of your requirements to ensure accurate and matching quotations.
helpCenter.platform_q5: How long does it take to receive a response after submitting an RFQ?
helpCenter.platform_a5: The platform commits to responding within 24 hours of receiving your RFQ. We will confirm the details of your requirements and provide a formal quotation as soon as possible based on the specifics.
helpCenter.platform_q6: What languages does the platform support?
helpCenter.platform_a6: The platform supports three languages: Chinese, English, and Swahili. You can switch languages at the top of the page. Product names, category names, and the system interface are all available in all three languages.
helpCenter.tools_subtitle: Selection and quality assessment of hand tools and power tool accessories
helpCenter.tools_q1: What are the common materials for hand tools? How do I choose?
helpCenter.tools_a1:
Common materials and their applications:
• Carbon Steel: High hardness, good value for money. Suitable for general-purpose wrenches, pliers, and screwdrivers. Downside: prone to rust — keep dry.
• Chrome Vanadium Steel (Cr-V): Balances strength and toughness. The mainstream material for mid-to-high-end hand tools, with better wear and corrosion resistance than carbon steel.
• Stainless Steel: Best rust resistance. Ideal for humid or chemical environments, but slightly lower hardness — not suitable for high-torque applications.
• Aluminum Alloy: Lightweight. Suitable for spirit levels, adjustable wrenches, and other tools where reducing weight matters.
Recommendation: For general construction site use, Cr-V offers the best value. For humid environments (coastal areas, plumbing work), prioritize stainless steel.
helpCenter.tools_q2: What parameters should I look at to ensure I'm buying quality hand tools?
helpCenter.tools_a2:
Key characteristics of quality tools:
• Surface Finish: Quality tools have smooth surfaces with even plating (matte chrome or bright chrome) and a refined feel.
• Opening Precision: Wrenches and sockets should be dimensionally accurate, fitting bolts snugly with no play.
• Handle Grip: Dual-color injection-molded rubber grips should bond firmly and reduce fatigue during extended use.
• Hardness Marking: Reputable products display hardness values (e.g., HRC 40-48) and material (e.g., Cr-V) stamped on the tool head.
• Brand Stamp: Clear brand name and specification markings are a basic assurance of quality.
All tools on BuildReach are sourced from manufacturers that have passed qualification audits. Product pages indicate material and hardness information for easy comparison.
helpCenter.tools_q3: How do I confirm compatibility for power tool accessories (drill bits, saw blades, etc.)?
helpCenter.tools_a3:
Check three key parameters:
• Interface Type: For drill bits, check the shank type (round shank / hex shank / SDS-plus). For saw blades, check the mounting hole diameter and outer diameter.
• Power Rating Match: The maximum RPM rated on the accessory must not be lower than the no-load speed of your power tool.
• Material & Application: Drill bits are categorized by material — HSS (high-speed steel) for metal, carbide-tipped for concrete, and wood bits for woodworking. Do not interchange them.
We recommend confirming the brand and model of your power tool before ordering. BuildReach customer service can assist with compatibility verification.
helpCenter.tools_q4: Are tool sets or individual tools a better deal?
helpCenter.tools_a4:
Sets are typically 20%–35% cheaper than buying items individually, and they come with a storage case for easy organization. Sets are ideal for:
• Basic tool outfitting for new construction sites or projects
• Standardized kits for maintenance teams
If you only need to supplement specific sizes (e.g., a particular socket size), buying individual items is more flexible.
helpCenter.tools_q5: How are tool products typically packaged?
helpCenter.tools_a5:
Standard packaging by tool type:
• Individual Tools: Blister card or poly bag packaging, then placed in cartons to prevent damage during transit.
• Tool Sets: Blow-molded storage case + outer carton, with each tool secured in a dedicated slot for easy on-site management.
• Ocean Freight: Suppliers use five-layer corrugated cartons with stretch film wrapping for moisture protection, ensuring safe long-distance transport.
BuildReach inspects packaging before shipment to ensure it meets cross-border shipping standards.
helpCenter.tools_q6: How do I evaluate the accuracy of measuring tools (tape measures, spirit levels, etc.)?
helpCenter.tools_a6:
• Tape Measures: Look for accuracy class markings (Class I or Class II) — Class I is more precise. A quality tape measure blade holds firm without buckling when extended to 2 meters, with sharp, clear markings.
• Spirit Levels: The key is bubble vial accuracy. A quality spirit level reads consistently in both horizontal and vertical positions and shows the same reading when flipped 180°. The aluminum body should be perfectly straight with no bowing.
• Laser Distance Meters: Check stated accuracy (typically ±1.5mm to ±2mm) and measuring range. For outdoor use, choose a model with laser enhancement mode.
helpCenter.tools_q7: What should I consider when purchasing abrasives (grinding wheels, cutting discs, etc.)?
helpCenter.tools_a7:
• Cutting discs and grinding wheels must have a maximum RPM rating — the operating speed must never exceed the rated value. This is a safety requirement.
• Select by material being processed: resin-bonded grinding discs for metal, diamond blades for stone/concrete, and flap discs for sanding.
• Outer diameter and mounting hole must match your angle grinder (common sizes: 100mm / 125mm / 180mm).
• Storage: Grinding wheels are moisture-sensitive. Store flat to prevent deformation. Shelf life is typically 3 years from manufacture.
helpCenter.tools_q8: How do I choose between pneumatic and electric tools?
helpCenter.tools_a8:
Comparison:
• Pneumatic Tools: Require an air compressor. Lower unit cost, stronger power output, longer lifespan, and overload-resistant. Best for workshops, auto repair shops, and other locations with a fixed air supply.
• Electric Tools: Plug-and-play, portable, and flexible. Best for construction sites and distributed work locations.
• Recommendation: If the work location is fixed and usage volume is high (e.g., assembly line fastening), pneumatic tools are more efficient and cost-effective. If work locations vary or mobility is needed (e.g., renovation sites), electric tools are more convenient.
helpCenter.safety_subtitle: Selection and standards for personal protective equipment (PPE) and safety devices
helpCenter.safety_q1: What standards apply to hard hats? How do I choose?
helpCenter.safety_a1:
Key international standards:
• EN 397 (European): Impact and penetration resistance — the most commonly adopted standard in East Africa.
• ANSI Z89.1 (US): Divided into Type I (top protection) and Type II (top + side protection).
Selection tips:
• Material: ABS is most common and cost-effective; PE offers better chemical resistance; fiberglass withstands high temperatures.
• Suspension System: 6-point or 8-point suspension is preferred for stability and comfort.
• Additional Features: Consider whether you need integrated visor slots, earmuff attachment points, or reflective strips.
Note: Hard hats have an expiration date — ABS models are typically valid for 3 years from the date of manufacture.
helpCenter.safety_q2: There are so many types of work gloves — how do I choose by application?
helpCenter.safety_a2:
Common materials matched to applications:
• Latex/Rubber Gloves: Waterproof and chemical-resistant. Suitable for cleaning and chemical handling.
• Nitrile Gloves: Oil, acid, and alkali resistant. Suitable for mechanical maintenance and painting.
• PU Palm-Coated Gloves: Thin and flexible. Suitable for electronics assembly and precision work.
• Cowhide Leather Gloves: Abrasion and heat resistant. Suitable for welding and handling rough materials.
• Cut-Resistant Gloves: HPPE or steel wire woven. Rated A–F per EN 388 (A = lowest, F = highest). Grade C or above is recommended for sheet metal work.
When selecting, first identify the primary risks in your work environment (cuts / burns / chemical contact / electrical hazard), then match the appropriate material.
helpCenter.safety_q3: How do I assess the grade and quality of safety shoes?
helpCenter.safety_a3:
Graded per EN ISO 20345:
• SB: Basic toe protection (200J impact-resistant steel toe)
• S1: Toe protection + antistatic + energy-absorbing heel
• S1P: S1 + puncture-resistant midsole
• S3: S1P + water-resistant upper + slip-resistant outsole
Construction sites generally require at least S1P (crush + puncture protection). For wet environments, choose S3.
Quality indicators:
• The toe cap should not visibly deform under pressure
• The sole should flex and spring back without cracking
• Interior stitching should be neat with no exposed threads
• Outsole tread depth should be sufficient (affects slip resistance)
helpCenter.safety_a4:
Classified per EN ISO 20471 into three levels:
• Class 1: Minimum visibility. Suitable for low-risk areas (e.g., inside warehouses).
• Class 2: Medium visibility. Suitable for moderate-traffic areas (e.g., parking lots).
• Class 3: Maximum visibility. Required for highways, nighttime construction, and other high-risk scenarios.
Higher classes have larger areas of fluorescent fabric and reflective tape. For road construction in East Africa, Class 2 or above is recommended. Reflective tape width should be at least 50mm — microprismatic tape is preferred over glass-bead tape for superior reflectivity.
helpCenter.safety_q5: What is the difference between dust masks and gas masks?
helpCenter.safety_a5:
• Dust Masks (FFP series): Filter solid particles and water-based aerosols only. FFP1 filters 80%, FFP2 filters 94%, FFP3 filters 99%. Suitable for dust, grinding, and cement mixing.
• Gas Masks (half-face/full-face respirators): Use replaceable filter cartridges to remove organic vapors, acidic gases, etc. Suitable for spray painting and chemical operations.
Key difference: Dust masks cannot protect against toxic gases. When working with paints, solvents, or chemicals, a gas mask is mandatory.
helpCenter.safety_q6: What parameters should I look at when purchasing security cameras?
helpCenter.safety_a6:
Key parameters:
• Resolution: 2MP (1080P) is the baseline; 4MP (2K) can capture facial details.
• Night Vision: Check infrared night vision range (common: 30m / 50m / 80m). Full-color night vision is preferred where possible.
• Protection Rating: Outdoor use requires at least IP66 (rain and dust proof). IP67 is recommended for East Africa's dusty conditions.
• Storage: Local NVR (Network Video Recorder) is more reliable. Cloud storage depends on network conditions.
• Power Supply: Standard PoE (Power over Ethernet) is most convenient — a single cable handles both data and power.
helpCenter.safety_q7: How do I select fire safety equipment (fire extinguishers, etc.)?
helpCenter.safety_a7:
Select by fire type:
• Class A (solid materials: wood, paper): Water-based or ABC dry powder extinguisher
• Class B (liquid fires: oil, solvents): Foam or CO₂ extinguisher
• Class C (gas fires): Dry powder extinguisher
• Class E (electrical fires): CO₂ extinguisher (non-conductive, won't damage equipment)
ABC dry powder extinguishers are recommended for construction sites (most versatile). Use CO₂ for electrical equipment rooms. Always check that the pressure gauge needle is in the green zone — expired extinguishers must be inspected or replaced.
helpCenter.safety_q8: Do PPE products have expiration dates? How do I verify they are in good condition?
helpCenter.safety_a8:
Reference shelf life for major PPE products:
• Hard Hats: ABS — 3 years; PE — 2 years (from date of manufacture). A certificate of conformity is included in the packaging.
• Safety Shoes: Normal service life is 1–2 years. Outsole rubber that retains its elasticity indicates good condition.
• Work Gloves: Latex/nitrile gloves have a 3-year shelf life when stored in sealed packaging.
• Fire Extinguishers: Dry powder extinguishers are valid for 5–8 years. A pressure gauge needle in the green zone indicates normal status.
BuildReach verifies production dates at the procurement stage to ensure all products shipped are within their usable life.
helpCenter.fasteners_subtitle: Selection and identification of bolts, nuts, screws, anchors, and sealants
helpCenter.fasteners_q1: What do bolt strength grades (e.g., 4.8, 8.8, 10.9) mean? How do I choose?
helpCenter.fasteners_a1:
Strength grades are stamped on the bolt head. Here's what they mean:
• The number before the decimal × 100 = tensile strength (MPa). For example, Grade 8.8 has a tensile strength ≥ 800 MPa.
• The number after the decimal × 10 = yield-to-tensile ratio (%). This means the yield strength is 80% of the tensile strength.
Common selections:
• Grade 4.8: Light-duty connections such as railings and light brackets
• Grade 8.8: Medium-to-high-strength connections such as steel structures and machinery — the most versatile
• Grade 10.9: High-strength connections such as bridges and heavy equipment
• Grade 12.9: Ultra-high-strength — for special engineering applications
For general construction site use, Grade 8.8 meets most needs. High-rise steel structures or load-bearing joints should use Grade 10.9 as specified by the design.
helpCenter.fasteners_q2: What surface treatment should I choose for fasteners used outdoors or in humid environments?
helpCenter.fasteners_a2:
Common surface treatments compared:
• Zinc Plated: The most basic corrosion protection. Low cost. Suitable for indoor use and short-term outdoor exposure.
• Hot-Dip Galvanized: Thick zinc coating with strong corrosion resistance. Suitable for long-term outdoor exposure and coastal regions.
• Dacromet: No hydrogen embrittlement risk. Superior corrosion protection compared to electroplated zinc. Suitable for high-strength bolts (Grade 10.9 and above).
• Stainless Steel (A2/304, A4/316): Best corrosion resistance. A4/316 is suitable for seawater-contact environments but is the most expensive.
For coastal cities in East Africa (e.g., Dar es Salaam) with high humidity and salt spray, outdoor fasteners should be at least hot-dip galvanized or stainless steel 304.
helpCenter.fasteners_q3: What is the difference between self-tapping screws and self-drilling screws?
helpCenter.fasteners_a3:
• Self-Tapping Screws: Require a pre-drilled pilot hole. The thread cuts its own path. Suitable for thin sheet metal, plastic, and wood.
• Self-Drilling Screws (also called TEK screws): Have a built-in drill point at the tip — no pre-drilling required. They penetrate steel plate directly. Ideal for metal roofing sheets and light steel framing, offering high installation efficiency.
Key selection tip: Choose the appropriate drill point length and thread pitch based on the thickness of the material being connected. For steel plates thicker than 5mm, pre-drilling with standard bolts is recommended over self-drilling screws.
helpCenter.fasteners_q4: How do I select expansion bolts/anchors? What drill hole sizes correspond to each?
helpCenter.fasteners_a4:
• Metal Expansion Anchors: Grip by expanding a sleeve against the hole wall. High load capacity. Suitable for concrete and solid brick walls.
• Plastic Expansion Plugs + Screws: Lower load capacity. Suitable for hollow bricks and lightweight walls.
• Chemical Anchors: Bond via chemical adhesive. Highest load capacity. Suitable for curtain walls and equipment foundations.
Drill hole size correspondence (metric):
• M6 expansion bolt → 8mm drill hole
• M8 expansion bolt → 10mm drill hole
• M10 expansion bolt → 12mm drill hole
• M12 expansion bolt → 16mm drill hole
Drill depth should exceed the anchor sleeve length by 10–15mm. Clean dust from the hole before installation.
helpCenter.fasteners_q5: What types of sealants are there? What is each type best suited for?
helpCenter.fasteners_a5:
Common types:
• Silicone Sealant: Excellent elasticity and weather resistance. Suitable for door/window gaps, bathroom waterproofing, and curtain wall joints. Available in acidic (stronger adhesion but corrodes metals) and neutral (safe for general use) formulas.
• Polyurethane Sealant: High hardness, paintable. Suitable for concrete expansion joints and floor joints.
• MS Modified Silicone Sealant: Best overall performance — solvent-free, paintable, eco-friendly. Suitable for high-end construction.
• Butyl Sealant: Never fully cures, maintains persistent tackiness. Suitable for metal roofing overlaps.
For general building door/window sealing, neutral silicone is sufficient. Use polyurethane for concrete/floor joints, and butyl for metal roofing.
helpCenter.fasteners_q6: How do I use parameters and markings to select quality fasteners?
helpCenter.fasteners_a6:
Key markings to look for when purchasing:
• Strength Grade Marking: Legitimate bolts have the grade (e.g., 8.8, 10.9) and manufacturer's mark stamped on the head — a basic assurance of quality.
• Thread Quality: Quality threads are smooth and uniform with consistent, well-defined profiles.
• Surface Treatment: Even, lustrous coating indicates proper anti-corrosion processing.
• Dimensional Accuracy: Reputable products have tight tolerances — an M8 bolt diameter is precisely controlled to 8mm ± 0.2mm.
• Supporting Documentation: For bulk purchases, you can request material certificates and mechanical property test reports.
BuildReach's partner fastener manufacturers maintain comprehensive quality inspection systems. Product pages list material and strength grade for easy selection.
helpCenter.fasteners_q7: What are the typical packaging specifications for fasteners? How do I estimate order quantities?
helpCenter.fasteners_a7:
Common packaging specifications:
• Small sizes (M4–M10): Packed by weight (kg) or quantity (per hundred/thousand) in small boxes, then placed in cartons.
• Large sizes (M12 and above): Individually packaged or in woven bags by quantity.
• High-strength bolts: Individually wrapped in oiled paper to protect threads and coatings.
Ordering quantity tips:
• Add 5%–10% surplus to your BOM (Bill of Materials) quantities to avoid reorders due to loss or damage.
• Small-size fasteners are more economical when purchased by weight (kg).
• Mixed-specification bulk orders may qualify for better pricing.
Include specific sizes and quantities in your RFQ notes, and BuildReach will recommend the most cost-effective procurement plan.
helpCenter.fasteners_q8: How do I choose between stainless steel bolts and zinc-plated carbon steel bolts?
helpCenter.fasteners_a8:
Comparison:
• Zinc-Plated Carbon Steel: High strength (available in 8.8/10.9/12.9 grades). Once the zinc coating is damaged, rust will develop. Best for indoor use or structures with protective coatings.
• Stainless Steel 304 (A2): Good corrosion resistance, but maximum strength is roughly equivalent to carbon steel Grade 5.6 (approx. 500 MPa). Suitable for general connections in coastal areas.
• Stainless Steel 316 (A4): Resistant to seawater corrosion. Ideal for ports, chemical plants, and other extreme corrosion environments. Highest cost.
Important: Do not mix stainless steel bolts with carbon steel nuts (galvanic corrosion). Always use matching materials throughout.
helpCenter.electrical_subtitle: Selection and safety essentials for cables, lighting, distribution boxes, conduits, and switches
helpCenter.electrical_q1: How do I select cable cross-section size based on load?
helpCenter.electrical_a1:
Safe current-carrying reference for common single-phase 220V copper cables:
• 1.5mm²: Approx. 10–15A, suitable for lighting circuits
• 2.5mm²: Approx. 16–25A, suitable for standard outlets
• 4mm²: Approx. 25–35A, suitable for air conditioners, water heaters, and other high-power appliances
• 6mm²: Approx. 32–45A, suitable for building main supply lines
• 10mm²: Approx. 45–65A, suitable for small distribution panel feeders
Selection principle: Calculate total load current (power ÷ voltage), then choose cable size from the current-carrying capacity chart with 20%–30% headroom. For long runs (>50m), increase one size to compensate for voltage drop.
helpCenter.electrical_q2: What is the difference between BV and BVR wire?
helpCenter.electrical_a2:
• BV (Single-Core Solid Wire): Single solid copper conductor. Suitable for fixed concealed wiring (in-wall conduit). Less flexible once bent, but joints are more secure.
• BVR (Multi-Strand Flexible Wire): Multiple fine copper strands twisted together. Soft and easy to bend. Suitable for applications requiring movement or frequent bending (distribution panel wiring, temporary circuits).
BV wire is the standard choice for residential and building in-wall conduit wiring. BVR is more practical for secondary wiring inside distribution panels.
helpCenter.electrical_q3: How do I assess LED lighting quality?
helpCenter.electrical_a3:
Key parameters:
• Luminous Efficacy (lm/W): Indicates lumens produced per watt. Quality LED fixtures exceed 100 lm/W; below 80 lm/W is considered low-end.
• Color Temperature: 3000K warm yellow (residential), 4000K natural white (office), 6500K cool white (workshops/industrial).
• Color Rendering Index (CRI/Ra): Ra > 80 is acceptable; Ra > 90 is needed for color-critical applications.
• LED Driver: Quality fixtures use isolated constant-current drivers with 30,000–50,000 hour lifespans. Cheap fixtures use capacitive droppers, which cause noticeable flicker and short lifespan.
• Heat Dissipation: Aluminum substrate + aluminum heat sink is superior to plastic housings — lower temperatures extend LED chip life.
helpCenter.electrical_q4: What should I consider when selecting a distribution box?
helpCenter.electrical_a4:
• Number of Circuits: Reserve 20%–30% extra slots beyond actual circuit count (for future expansion).
• Enclosure Material: Indoor — cold-rolled steel with powder coating. Outdoor/humid — stainless steel 304 or SMC fiberglass.
• Protection Rating: Indoor — IP30 to IP44 is sufficient. Outdoor — at least IP55. Dusty workshops — IP65.
• Cable Entry: Top-in/top-out or bottom-in/bottom-out, depending on site wiring layout.
• Breaker Selection: Main breaker should be a Molded Case Circuit Breaker (MCCB). Branch circuits use Miniature Circuit Breakers (MCB) + Residual Current Device (RCD).
• Busbar Capacity: Must match the total incoming current — don't just size by breaker rating alone.
helpCenter.electrical_q5: Should I choose PVC or metal conduit?
helpCenter.electrical_a5:
• PVC Conduit: Lightweight, good insulation, corrosion-resistant. Suitable for indoor concealed wiring. Downsides: not heat-resistant and limited compressive strength.
• Galvanized Steel Conduit (JDG/KBG): High strength with good fire resistance. Suitable for surface-mounted and mechanically protected installations. JDG (set-screw type) has thicker walls; KBG (compression type) has thinner walls but faster installation.
• Stainless Steel Flexible Conduit: Good flexibility. Suitable for equipment connection sections and routes with many bends.
PVC is adequate for residential in-wall concealed wiring. Commercial surface-mounted wiring and fire protection circuits require metal conduit.
helpCenter.electrical_q6: What should I look for when selecting switches and outlets?
helpCenter.electrical_a6:
• Rated Current: 10A is sufficient for standard switches. High-power appliances (air conditioners, water heaters) require 16A or 20A dedicated outlets.
• Contact Material: Silver alloy contacts (AgCdO or AgNi) offer good conductivity and long life. Copper contacts oxidize easily.
• Housing Material: Quality products use PC (polycarbonate) — flame retardant and color-stable. Low-quality products use urea resin, which yellows and becomes brittle.
• Safety Certification: Check for certification relevant to the target market.
• Feel Test: Quality switches have a crisp, firm click with positive feedback. Low-quality ones feel loose with weak spring-back.
helpCenter.electrical_q7: What should I consider when purchasing industrial controls (circuit breakers, contactors, relays)?
helpCenter.electrical_a7:
• Rated Parameter Matching: Circuit breaker rated current and breaking capacity must meet or exceed circuit requirements. Contactor rated current should be at least 1.25× the controlled motor's rated current.
• Brand Consistency: Using the same brand throughout a distribution panel ensures better compatibility for accessories (auxiliary contacts, undervoltage trip units, etc.).
• Model Number Coding: Industrial control product model numbers define the full specification and function set. Always verify the complete model number — not just the rated current.
• Anti-Counterfeit Verification: Major brands provide authentication codes. Always verify before placing bulk orders.
helpCenter.electrical_q8: How do I verify cable and wire quality when purchasing?
helpCenter.electrical_a8:
Key quality indicators to check:
• Packaging: Residential wire (BV/BVR) typically comes in 100m rolls, each with a compliance label showing specification, length, and production date.
• Insulation Quality: Quality cable has uniform insulation thickness, consistent color, and clear printing. It should flex without whitening.
• Copper Core Quality: Pure copper conductor cross-sections appear reddish-purple and lustrous, indicating optimal conductivity.
• Accurate Length: Reputable products have length deviation no greater than ±0.5%.
• Certification Markings: Products should bear complete specification, model, and manufacturer information.
All cables supplied through BuildReach come from fully certified manufacturers. Product pages provide detailed specifications for easy comparison.
helpCenter.doors_subtitle: Selection and quality assessment for doors/windows, plumbing fittings, and sanitary ceramics
helpCenter.doors_q1: How do I choose door and window profiles (aluminum alloy vs. PVC)?
helpCenter.doors_a1:
• Aluminum Alloy Windows & Doors: High strength, warp-resistant, long lifespan. The mainstream choice for commercial and mid-to-high-end residential buildings. Check wall thickness: national standards require main profiles ≥1.4mm (updated standard ≥1.8mm) for exterior windows — thinner profiles lack adequate strength.
• PVC-U (UPVC) Windows & Doors: Better thermal insulation than aluminum, at a lower price. Suitable for regions with large temperature swings but low wind pressure. Downside: limited UV resistance — prolonged sun exposure causes aging and discoloration.
• Thermal Break Aluminum: Uses insulating strips (PA66 nylon) between the aluminum sections to break the thermal bridge, combining aluminum's strength with PVC's insulation. A premium option.
East Africa's hot climate and intense sunlight favor aluminum alloy or thermal break aluminum. PVC ages faster in regions with strong UV exposure.
helpCenter.doors_q2: What should I look for when purchasing door and window hardware (hinges, handles, rollers)?
helpCenter.doors_a2:
Selection tips by component:
• Hinges: 304 stainless steel is recommended. They should open and close smoothly without noise. Quality hinges support 40–60 kg.
• Handles: Zinc alloy handles offer good weight and feel. The plated surface should be smooth and refined.
• Rollers (for sliding doors/windows): Dual-wheel designs are more stable than single-wheel. Bearing rollers are quieter and more durable. Choose stainless steel housing.
• Weatherstripping: EPDM (ethylene propylene diene monomer) rubber offers better elasticity and aging resistance than PVC strips.
Quality hardware should withstand 100,000+ open/close cycles. BuildReach product pages list material and key parameters to help you match by application.
helpCenter.doors_q3: Should I choose brass or stainless steel for faucets and valves?
helpCenter.doors_a3:
• Brass (H59/H62): Excellent castability and natural antibacterial properties — the traditional mainstream material. Quality brass faucets contain ≥59% copper with chrome-plated surfaces. Caution: Low-quality brass faucets may have high lead content. Look for "low-lead" labeling or NSF certification.
• Stainless Steel (304): Lead-free and healthy, corrosion-resistant, but more complex to cast with fewer design options. Ideal for health-conscious and durability-focused applications.
• Zinc Alloy: Corrodes easily and has a short lifespan. Not recommended for drinking water systems.
Quality check: A quality faucet feels heavy in hand. The chrome plating should be mirror-bright with no orange peel texture.
helpCenter.doors_q4: Where should PPR pipes and PVC pipes be used?
helpCenter.doors_a4:
• PPR Pipe (Polypropylene): Heat-resistant (continuous service temperature 70°C). Used for indoor hot and cold water supply lines. Connected by heat fusion welding — joints have zero leakage risk.
• PVC-U Pipe: Not heat-resistant (<45°C). Used for drainage, rainwater, and electrical conduit. Connected by solvent cement. Lower cost than PPR.
• PE Pipe: Good flexibility and cold resistance. Commonly used for outdoor main water supply lines (larger diameters).
Simple rule: Use PPR for water supply (especially hot water), PVC for drainage, and PE for large-diameter outdoor water mains.
helpCenter.doors_q5: How do I select quality sanitary ceramics (toilets, wash basins)?
helpCenter.doors_a5:
Key selection criteria:
• Ceramic Density: Quality ceramics produce a clear, resonant sound when tapped. The glaze should be smooth and fine with a mirror-like finish. Water absorption rate <0.5% indicates thorough firing.
• Flush Type (toilets): Siphon flush is quieter — suitable for residential use. Wash-down flush has stronger force — suitable for public facilities.
• Flush Mechanism: The inlet and drain valves inside the tank determine long-term reliability. Choose reputable valve component brands.
• Dimension Matching: Confirm the rough-in distance (distance from drain center to wall) — common sizes are 300mm and 400mm.
BuildReach provides detailed product specifications and photos to help you accurately match project requirements.
helpCenter.doors_q6: How do I select pipe fittings (elbows, tees, valves)?
helpCenter.doors_a6:
• Fitting Material Should Match the Pipe: PPR pipes with PPR fittings, stainless steel pipes with stainless steel fittings — to avoid galvanic corrosion.
• Brass Fittings (ball valves, gate valves): Choose forged brass (not cast) for higher pressure ratings. Surface should be clean and wall thickness uniform.
• Valve Selection: Ball valves for shutoff (fast operation, good seal); gate or globe valves for flow regulation; check valves to prevent backflow.
• Sealing Method: Threaded connections require PTFE tape. Flanged connections require appropriately sized rubber gaskets.
• Pressure Rating: PN16 (1.6 MPa) is sufficient for residential plumbing. Industrial piping should be selected based on system pressure.
helpCenter.doors_q7: How do I assess shower head quality and spray performance?
helpCenter.doors_a7:
• Material: Brass body is best (heavy, corrosion-resistant). Face plate can be stainless steel or ABS. Hoses should be stainless steel braided — they outlast all-plastic hoses.
• Spray Modes: Multi-function shower heads (rain, waterfall, massage) provide a better experience. The key is smooth switching between modes.
• Self-Cleaning Nozzles: Silicone nozzle tips resist limescale buildup and clean easily with a finger wipe.
• Thermostatic Feature: Thermostatic valve showers automatically maintain water temperature. More expensive but significantly better experience, especially for households with elderly or children.
• Water Pressure Requirement: Some large overhead rain showers require higher pressure (0.15 MPa or above). Verify if upper floors or low-pressure areas can supply adequate flow.
helpCenter.doors_q8: How do I choose door locks? What's the difference between mechanical and electronic locks?
helpCenter.doors_a8:
• Mechanical Locks: Simple and reliable — no batteries or power needed. Security grades: Grade A (cross-key, high mutual-opening rate), Grade B (crescent-shaped double-row pins), Grade C (blade cylinder, resists picking for >270 minutes). At minimum, choose Grade B.
• Electronic Smart Locks: Multiple unlock methods — fingerprint, PIN, card, and key. Highly convenient, but note: battery life (quality lithium batteries last approximately 6–12 months) and whether there is an emergency mechanical key override.
• Lock Body Material: 304 stainless steel is the most durable. Zinc alloy is cheaper but less resistant to forced entry.
• Installation Compatibility: Before purchasing, confirm door thickness (standard 40–50mm) and lock body specifications (cylinder length, spindle size).
helpCenter.decoration_subtitle: Selection of waterproofing materials, paints & coatings, decorative boards, and tiles
helpCenter.decoration_q1: What types of waterproofing materials are there? How do I choose by location?
helpCenter.decoration_a1:
Common types and where to use them:
• Polyurethane Waterproof Coating: Excellent elasticity. Suitable for bathrooms, kitchens, and other indoor wet areas. Can be applied on damp substrates with strong bonding.
• Acrylic Waterproof Coating: Water-based and eco-friendly with easy application. Suitable for balconies, kitchens, and smaller areas.
• SBS Modified Bitumen Membrane: Used for large-area roof waterproofing. Performs well across temperature extremes — suitable for East Africa's hot climate.
• Cementitious Crystalline Waterproofing: Penetrates concrete micropores and forms crystals to block water channels. Suitable for basements and water tanks.
Application principles: Bathroom waterproofing should extend at least 30cm up the wall (1.8m in shower areas). Water retention testing should last no less than 24 hours.
helpCenter.decoration_q2: How do I choose paint and coatings? What parameters indicate quality?
helpCenter.decoration_a2:
Key parameters for selecting coatings:
• Coverage: Quality latex paint covers the base color in a single coat, improving application efficiency.
• Scrub Resistance: Quality products withstand >10,000 scrub cycles for better durability.
• Environmental Rating: Lower VOC (Volatile Organic Compounds) is better — quality water-based paints have VOC <50g/L. Formaldehyde content should be <50mg/kg.
• Environmental Certifications: Blue Angel (Germany) and French A+ are widely recognized high-standard certifications.
• Can Inspection: Quality paint has uniform consistency, no lumps, and a mild odor.
BuildReach's partner coating manufacturers provide complete test reports and environmental certifications, available for review on product pages.
helpCenter.decoration_q3: How do I select tiles? What types suit different scenarios?
helpCenter.decoration_a3:
Core tile selection parameters:
• Water Absorption Rate: Floor tiles should be <0.5% (full-body porcelain) for high strength and wear resistance. Wall tiles can be up to <10%.
• Flatness: Place two tiles face to face — the smaller the gap, the flatter they are, and the better the installation result.
• Slip Resistance: For wet areas, choose tiles with a friction coefficient >0.5.
• Glaze Hardness: Quality glazed surfaces resist scratching and are easy to clean.
Recommendations by scenario:
• Outdoor/Balcony: Anti-slip tiles, water absorption <0.5%
• Bathroom Floor: Small anti-slip tiles (300×300mm) for easier drainage slope
• Living Room/Lobby: 800×800mm or 600×1200mm fully polished glazed or marble-look porcelain tiles
• Kitchen Wall: Glazed tiles — easy to wipe clean from grease
BuildReach organizes products by size and application, allowing you to filter quickly by project scenario.
helpCenter.decoration_q4: What are the differences between board types (plywood, MDF, melamine board)?
helpCenter.decoration_a4:
• Plywood: Cross-laminated wood veneer sheets hot-pressed together. High strength. Suitable for furniture frames and packing crates. Graded E0/E1 by formaldehyde emission — E0 is more eco-friendly.
• MDF (Medium Density Fiberboard): Wood fiber pulped and hot-pressed. Smooth surface, easy to process. Suitable for veneered surfaces and lacquered door panels. Downside: moisture-sensitive — swelling from water exposure is irreversible.
• Particle Board: Wood particles hot-pressed. Lowest cost but weakest strength and moisture resistance.
• OSB (Oriented Strand Board): Higher structural strength than standard particle board. Commonly used for wall sheathing and subfloor panels.
• Melamine Board (Pre-laminated Board): Core material + melamine-faced surface. Ready to use without painting. Suitable for cabinet panels.
Key selection factors: Clarify the purpose (structural / decorative / cabinet), check environmental grade (E0 preferred), and moisture content (<14%).
helpCenter.decoration_q5: What is the difference between exterior wall paint and interior latex paint?
helpCenter.decoration_a5:
• Exterior Paint: Strong weather resistance (UV-resistant, rain-resistant), good elasticity (accommodates thermal expansion/contraction), and high mildew/algae resistance. Given East Africa's abundant sunlight and rainfall, exterior paint should have a weather resistance lifespan of >8 years.
• Interior Latex Paint: Prioritizes environmental safety (low VOC, low odor), coverage, and texture. UV resistance is not needed, but scrub resistance is important.
• Not Interchangeable: Interior paint used outdoors will quickly chalk and peel. Exterior paint used indoors may not meet environmental standards (different resin formulations).
• Don't Skip Primer: Whether interior or exterior, primer seals alkalinity and improves adhesion. Skipping primer often leads to peeling and uneven finish.
helpCenter.decoration_q6: What types of insulation materials are available?
helpCenter.decoration_a6:
Common types:
• XPS (Extruded Polystyrene): Closed-cell structure with extremely low water absorption and high strength. Suitable for floor, roof insulation, and cold storage.
• EPS (Expanded Polystyrene): Open-cell structure with good breathability. Suitable for exterior wall insulation systems.
• Rock Wool Board: Class A non-combustible with good sound insulation. Suitable for building facades, partition walls, and pipe insulation where fire resistance is required. Downside: moisture-sensitive — requires proper moisture barrier.
• Glass Wool: Lightweight with excellent thermal and acoustic insulation. Commonly used for HVAC ducts, suspended ceilings, and steel structure building insulation.
• Rubber-Plastic Insulation Tube/Board: Good flexibility. Suitable for pipe insulation (HVAC lines, chilled water pipes).
Selection principles: First check fire rating requirements (Class A non-combustible — choose rock wool or glass wool), then thermal performance (lower thermal conductivity is better), and finally installation convenience.
helpCenter.decoration_q7: What is the difference between grout and tile filler?
helpCenter.decoration_a7:
• Tile Filler (White Cement-Based): Traditional joint-filling material. Prone to mold and discoloration, and changes color when wet. Suitable for commercial/industrial projects where aesthetics are not a priority.
• Epoxy Grout (Epoxy Resin-Based): Smooth, porcelain-like finish. Waterproof, mold-resistant, and color-stable with a wide range of color options. Suitable for bathrooms, kitchens, and areas requiring both waterproofing and visual appeal.
• Application Difference: Tile filler is simply pressed into joints. Epoxy grout should be applied 1–2 days after the filler base layer (otherwise it sinks). It is applied with a specialized grout gun.
• Cost Comparison: Epoxy grout costs approximately 5–8× more per unit than tile filler, but its maintenance cycle is much longer — more cost-effective in the long run.
helpCenter.decoration_q8: How are decorative building materials packaged and protected during shipping?
helpCenter.decoration_a8:
Standard packaging by category:
• Tiles: Packed on wooden frames or in wooden crates with cardboard separators between layers. Securely fastened for ocean freight to prevent shifting and chipping.
• Paints & Coatings: Sealed in drums/pails with MSDS (Material Safety Data Sheet). Solvent-based products are shipped via certified hazardous materials channels.
• Boards/Panels: Stacked flat with angle steel or timber edge protectors, covered with moisture-proof film.
• Waterproof Membranes: Stored and shipped upright to prevent compression deformation.
• Insulation Materials: Compressed and baled to reduce volume — they recover naturally upon unpacking.
BuildReach inspects packaging for every shipment before dispatch to ensure compliance with ocean freight standards. For large-format building materials, we also provide container loading plans to maximize container space utilization.
